source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two passive two-port networks are connected in cascade as shown in figure. A voltage source is connected at port 1. Given \( V_1 = A_1V_2 + B_1I_2 \), \( I_1 = C_1V_2 + D_1I_2 \), \( V_2 = A_2V_3 + B_2I_3 \), \( I_2 = C_2V_3 + D_2I_3 \), \( A_1, B_1, C_1, D_1, A_2, B_2, C_2 \) and \( D_2 \) are the generalized circuit constants. If the Thevenin equivalent circuit at port 3 consists of a voltage source \( V_T \) and impedance \( Z_T \) connected in series, then

open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text: Two passive two-port networks are connected in cascade as shown in figure. A voltage source is connected at port 1. Given \( V_1 = A_1V_2 + B_1I_2 \), \( I_1 = C_1V_2 + D_1I_2 \), \( V_2 = A_2V_3 + B_2I_3 \), and \( I_2 = C_2V_3 + D_2I_3 \), where \( A_1, B_1, C_1, D_1, A_2, B_2, C_2 \), and \( D_2 \) are the generalized circuit constants. If the Thevenin equivalent circuit at port 3 consists of a voltage source \( V_T \) and impedance \( Z_T \) connected in series, what is the expression for \( Z_T \)?
